# react-native-webrtc-kaleidoscope

> Live video effects for `react-native-webrtc`, packaged as a managed-Expo-friendly Expo Module. v0.1 ships `mirror` and `blur`; the architecture supports BYO effects in later versions.

## What this package is

`react-native-webrtc-kaleidoscope` is a thin layer over `react-native-webrtc`'s undocumented `track._setVideoEffects(['name'])` API. On native (iOS, Android), it registers named frame-processor factories at app boot via an Expo config plugin. On web, it uses `MediaStreamTrackProcessor` + `MediaStreamTrackGenerator` (Insertable Streams) behind the same JS facade.

It is **not** a fork of `react-native-webrtc`. It is **not** a managed-cloud SaaS like Fishjam Cloud. It is a small, self-contained module that consumers install alongside `react-native-webrtc` (peer dep) to get background blur and similar effects on local video tracks.

## Install

```sh
bun add react-native-webrtc react-native-webrtc-kaleidoscope
```

Add the config plugin to `app.config.ts`:

```ts
plugins: ['react-native-webrtc', 'react-native-webrtc-kaleidoscope'];
```

Run `bunx expo prebuild` to materialize the native registration calls.

## Use

```ts
import { applyVideoEffects } from 'react-native-webrtc-kaleidoscope';

const stream = await mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true });
const [track] = stream.getVideoTracks();
applyVideoEffects(track, ['blur']);
```

Pass `[]` to clear effects. Effects chain in array order.

## Public API

- `applyVideoEffects(track, names)` — apply zero or more named effects to a local `MediaStreamTrack`. Throws if the track is remote, or if the installed `react-native-webrtc` does not expose `_setVideoEffects`.
- `EffectName` — TypeScript union of the built-in effect names. v0.1: `'mirror' | 'blur'`.

## Effects shipped in v0.1

- `mirror` — horizontal flip. No ML. Pixel-exact across platforms.
- `blur` — person-segmentation + Gaussian background blur. Uses Apple Vision (iOS), MLKit Selfie Segmentation (Android), MediaPipe Selfie Segmentation WASM (web). Visual parity is "similar enough"; mask differences are accepted.

## Platform support

- **iOS** native ≥ 15.0 (Apple Vision person segmentation requirement)
- **Android** native ≥ API 24 (MLKit minimum)
- **Web** Chrome / Edge (any browser supporting `MediaStreamTrackProcessor`). Safari and Firefox lacking Insertable Streams produce a typed runtime error; capability-check before calling.

## Reference

Sibling projects [chromonym](https://github.com/simiancraft/chromonym) and [unitforge](https://github.com/simiancraft/unitforge) share this project's OSS-hygiene template.
